subject: music
description: Musical notes, staff, clefs, note values, accidentals

questions.question: How many lines does a musical staff have?
questions.explanation: A standard musical staff consists of 5 horizontal lines and 4 spaces.

questions.question: What are the note names in order from C?
options: C D E F G A B
A B C D E F G
C E G B D F A
D E F G A B C
questions.correct_answer: C D E F G A B
questions.explanation: Starting from C: C, D, E, F, G, A, B (then back to C).

questions.question: What clef is also known as the 'G clef'?
questions.explanation: The treble clef curls around the G line (second line from bottom).

questions.question: What clef is also known as the 'F clef'?
questions.explanation: The bass clef's two dots surround the F line (fourth line from bottom).

questions.question: How many beats does a whole note get in 4/4 time?
questions.explanation: A whole note lasts for 4 beats in common (4/4) time.

questions.question: How many beats does a half note get in 4/4 time?
questions.explanation: A half note gets 2 beats — half the duration of a whole note.

questions.question: What does a sharp (♯) do to a note?
options: Lowers it by a half step
Raises it by a half step
questions.correct_answer: Raises it by a half step
questions.explanation: A sharp raises the pitch by one semitone (half step).

questions.question: What does a flat (♭) do to a note?
options: Raises it by a half step
Lowers it by a half step
questions.correct_answer: Lowers it by a half step
questions.explanation: A flat lowers the pitch by one semitone (half step).

questions.question: The spaces on the treble clef spell which word?
questions.explanation: From bottom to top, the spaces of the treble clef are F, A, C, E — spelling FACE.

questions.question: The lines of the treble clef from bottom to top are:
questions.explanation: Every Good Boy Does Fine — E, G, B, D, F.

questions.question: How many beats does a quarter note get in 4/4 time?
questions.explanation: A quarter note gets 1 beat in 4/4 time.

questions.question: What does a dot after a note do?
questions.explanation: A dotted half note = 2 + 1 = 3 beats. The dot adds 50% of the note's value.

questions.question: What is a natural sign (♮) used for?
options: To cancel a sharp or flat
questions.correct_answer: To cancel a sharp or flat
questions.explanation: A natural sign cancels any previous sharp or flat on that note.

questions.question: How many eighth notes equal one quarter note?
questions.explanation: Two eighth notes have the same total duration as one quarter note.

questions.question: What is Middle C?
options: The lowest note on a piano
The note between treble and bass clef staves
The highest note on a guitar
questions.correct_answer: The note between treble and bass clef staves
questions.explanation: Middle C sits on a ledger line between the treble and bass clef staves.

questions.question: What is enharmonic equivalence?
options: Two notes with different names but same pitch
Two notes played at the same time
questions.correct_answer: Two notes with different names but same pitch
questions.explanation: C♯ and D♭ are enharmonic equivalents — different names, same sound.

questions.question: How many half steps are in an octave?
questions.explanation: There are 12 semitones (half steps) in one octave.

questions.question: What is a ledger line?
options: A line added above or below the staff for notes outside its range
questions.correct_answer: A line added above or below the staff for notes outside its range
questions.explanation: Ledger lines extend the staff to notate pitches above or below the five lines.

questions.question: What is a tie in music notation?
options: A curved line connecting two notes of the same pitch
questions.correct_answer: A curved line connecting two notes of the same pitch
questions.explanation: A tie connects two notes of the same pitch, combining their durations.

questions.question: How many sixteenth notes equal one quarter note?
questions.explanation: 4 sixteenth notes = 1 quarter note in duration.

questions.question: What is a double sharp (𝄪)?
options: Raises a note by two half steps
Raises a note by one half step
Lowers a note by two half steps
Makes a note twice as loud
questions.correct_answer: Raises a note by two half steps
questions.explanation: A double sharp raises the note by a whole step (two semitones).

questions.question: In concert pitch, what note does a B♭ trumpet play to sound a C?
questions.explanation: A B♭ trumpet must play a D (a whole step up) to produce concert C.

questions.question: What is the frequency of concert A (A4)?
questions.explanation: Concert A (A4) is internationally standardized at 440 Hz.

questions.question: What does 'pp' mean in musical dynamics?
questions.explanation: 'pp' stands for pianissimo, meaning very soft/quiet.

questions.question: What is a tremolo?
options: A rapid repetition of a note or alternation between two notes
questions.correct_answer: A rapid repetition of a note or alternation between two notes
questions.explanation: Tremolo is the rapid reiteration of a single note or alternation between two notes.
